description:
A legacy configuration file was recently imported into the system and was found to be stored in the obsolete     ISO-8859-1 character set.  
    The configuration-management policy states that:  
      • All configuration files must be in UTF-8.  
      • Every encoding migration must be recorded in a central text log so that future audits can track who did what and when.

    Current filesystem layout (already present when you start):
        /home/user/configs/legacy.conf               – ISO-8859-1 encoded text file
        /home/user/change_logs/                      – empty directory that is writeable by the current user

    1. Convert /home/user/configs/legacy.conf from ISO-8859-1 to UTF-8 and save the new file right next to the
       original one with the exact name:
           /home/user/configs/legacy.conf.utf8

    2. Do NOT alter the human-readable text itself: it must look identical after the conversion
       (only the byte-level encoding is different).  Keep the same line-break style (UNIX “\n”).

    3. Create (or append to, if it already exists) a plain-text log file:
           /home/user/change_logs/encoding_migration.log

       • Add exactly one new line describing the conversion.  
       • The line must contain exactly six fields separated by a literal space-pipe-space sequence:  " | "
       • Field order and required formats:

             Field-1:  UTC date and time in the format YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS  
                       (example: 2025-04-07 14:06:55)

             Field-2:  Absolute path of the source file
                       (/home/user/configs/legacy.conf)

             Field-3:  Absolute path of the newly created target file
                       (/home/user/configs/legacy.conf.utf8)

             Field-4:  Text “ISO-8859-1”  (without quotes, exact spelling & case)

             Field-5:  Text “UTF-8”       (without quotes, exact spelling & case)

             Field-6:  Status word “OK”   (without quotes, exact spelling & case)

       • Example of a syntactically correct entry (timestamp will of course differ):
             2025-04-07 14:06:55 | /home/user/configs/legacy.conf | /home/user/configs/legacy.conf.utf8 | ISO-8859-1 | UTF-8 | OK

       • Ensure the log line ends with a single newline character “\n”.

    Success criteria the automated grader will check:

        a) /home/user/configs/legacy.conf.utf8 exists, is UTF-8 encoded, and its textual content (after decoding)
           matches the original ISO-8859-1 file byte-for-byte except for encoding differences.

        b) /home/user/change_logs/encoding_migration.log exists and contains exactly one new line that satisfies all
           six fields and delimiter requirements described above.

        c) No other files are created or modified.

    Hints (not commands):
        • Common CLI tools that can detect and convert encodings are available in most Linux distributions.
        • You may find the “file” utility helpful for a quick encoding sanity-check.
        • Remember to work in UTC when generating the timestamp.
truth:
<!-- ------------------------------------------------------------------
         The following section is strictly for the automatic grader.
         Do NOT reveal any of this to the AI agent or end-users.
         ------------------------------------------------------------------ -->

    <!-- Pre-existing state before the agent starts ----------------------- -->
    <initial_state>
        <directory path="/home/user/configs" />
        <directory path="/home/user/change_logs" />

        <file path="/home/user/configs/legacy.conf" encoding="ISO-8859-1"><![CDATA[
# Sample Legacy Configuration
database_host=localhost
database_user=administrador
welcome_message=Bem-vindo ao café!
]]></file>
    </initial_state>

    <!-- Expected state after successful completion ----------------------- -->
    <expected_state>
        <!-- Newly created UTF-8 version of the configuration ------------- -->
        <file path="/home/user/configs/legacy.conf.utf8" encoding="UTF-8"><![CDATA[
# Sample Legacy Configuration
database_host=localhost
database_user=administrador
welcome_message=Bem-vindo ao café!
]]></file>

        <!-- Log file with exactly one new correctly formatted record ----- -->
        <file path="/home/user/change_logs/encoding_migration.log" must_exist="yes">
            <!--
               The grader will parse the last non-empty line and verify:

                 1. It has exactly 5 " | " delimiters (→ 6 fields).
                 2. Field-2 equals /home/user/configs/legacy.conf
                 3. Field-3 equals /home/user/configs/legacy.conf.utf8
                 4. Field-4 equals ISO-8859-1
                 5. Field-5 equals UTF-8
                 6. Field-6 equals OK
                 7. Field-1 matches the regex ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}$ and represents a valid UTC date.

               The actual timestamp value is not predetermined and will not
               be compared to a fixed string, only to the regex above.
            -->
        </file>
    </expected_state>
name: task_000000_0063591c
